topic: A person finds their spouse's secret savings account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The slip of paper was tucked behind a loose floorboard in the spare room, a place I hadn't dared to look since the move. My fingers, slick with a cold sweat, traced the edge of the brittle note. It wasn't a bill or a forgotten grocery list; it was a bank statement. My breath hitched in my throat, a sharp, painful catch. The numbers swam before my eyes—figures that dwarfed everything we had ever planned, a hidden accumulation that felt like a ticking time bomb. A sudden, dizzying wave washed over me, making the room tilt. I quickly crumpled the paper, shoving it deep into my pocket, my heart hammering against my ribs like a trapped bird desperate for escape. Every shadow in the hallway seemed to stretch and writhe.

[story 2]

I found the hidden access code taped beneath the antique clock in the study. The air in the room suddenly felt thick, suffocating, as if the oxygen had been sucked out. When I finally logged into the account, the balance stared back at me—a sum so immense it made my hands shake uncontrollably. I stared at the screen, my vision tunneling. A low, involuntary sound escaped my lips, a choked whisper of disbelief. My palms began to dampen, and I had to press them hard against my thighs to keep from trembling entirely. The silence of the house amplified the frantic drumming in my ears, every creak of the old floorboards sounding like an accusation. I needed to close the laptop, needed to erase the evidence, but my muscles refused to obey.

[story 3]

It was tucked inside the lining of my spouse's favorite winter coat, a small, unassuming envelope. When I finally opened it, the contents—a series of encrypted transaction confirmations—made the world narrow down to just that single, horrifying reality. My stomach clenched into a painful knot. I stood rigid by the window, watching the streetlights blur through the rain-streaked glass. My jaw locked so tightly I could feel the strain in my molars. I took a slow, shallow breath, trying to regulate the frantic rhythm of my pulse. The implications of this secret wealth pressed down on me, heavy and suffocating, leaving me utterly paralyzed, unable to move toward any logical conclusion.
